title: Antioxidant Status. Plasma Antioxidant Capacity Changes Following a Meal as a Measure of the Ability of a Food to Alter In Vivo Antioxidant Status. This pilot human study examined whether consuming grapes with a meal could prevent post-prandial oxidative stress, a natural state of oxidative stress in the body that results from eating a meal containing just protein, carbohydrates, and fat, with no antioxidants. The results indicated grape consumption increased blood antioxidant capacity and helped prevent post-meal oxidative stress.

sentences.text: Plasma Antioxidant Capacity Changes Following a Meal as a Measure of the Ability of a Food to Alter In Vivo Antioxidant Status.

This pilot human study examined whether consuming grapes with a meal could prevent post-prandial oxidative stress, a natural state of oxidative stress in the body that results from eating a meal containing just protein, carbohydrates, and fat, with no antioxidants.
The results indicated grape consumption increased blood antioxidant capacity and helped prevent post-meal oxidative stress.
